articles.title: A 250-Year-Old Volcano Machine Finally Erupts!
articles.summary: A mechanical volcano imagined in 1775 has been built for the first time. Two engineering students used lasers and LEDs to make it erupt with light. You can see it at a library in Melbourne until June 28, 2026.

articles.title: Why Is Math Harder for Some Kids? Brain Scans Show a Surprising Clue
articles.summary: When kids with math troubles see number symbols, their brains work differently. But show them dots instead, and the difference disappears. Scientists found two brain areas that might explain why.

articles.title: AI Beats Doctors at Diagnosing Patients—in a Real Hospital Test!
articles.summary: An AI model diagnosed patients better than two experienced doctors in a real emergency room. It used only text from health records. The AI even solved a tricky case involving lupus that doctors missed.
